behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Correctness here depends on what the real LLM produces from natural-language requirements. A mocked model can validate parsing/validation logic, but it can miss real signature misunderstandings and variability in model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with a real LLM on a prompt that precisely specifies parameter names, types, and return type; assert the emitted function signature matches exactly. Repeat across multiple runs/seeds to detect stochastic signature drift.

behaviors.description: The generated implementation passes all provided test cases on the first or a subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary includes executing generated code against a real test harness (filesystem/subprocess/runtime behavior). Pure mocks of execution can hide failures in compilation, imports, environment, and runner integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real code-execution path (write generated code to disk and run the actual test suite via the service’s runner). Drive scenarios where attempt 1 fails and attempt 2 or 3 passes; assert final success only when the real suite reports all tests green.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement step includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from its mistakes.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily wiring between test-result collection and prompt construction. A unit test of a formatter is too narrow; you need the retry orchestrator and test-output plumbing together to catch dropped/truncated failure context.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger an initial failing attempt, capture the exact next prompt sent to the model client, and assert it contains the concrete failure messages (e.g., assertion text/trace snippets) from the prior test run.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: The main risk is orchestration state/control-flow: retry counting, stop condition, and error payload assembly. This requires multiple components together; pure unit tests can miss cross-component state bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Force three consecutive failing attempts (deterministic failing generations or failing tests), then assert no 4th attempt occurs and the returned error includes exactly three attempt records with their associated test results.

behaviors.description: The full loop — generate, test, refine, test again — produces a working implementation for a function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence, with later model behavior depending on earlier failures. Single-step agentic tests or mocked retries will miss emergent loop behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario with a real LLM and real test execution on a known hard function where one-shot generation often fails. Assert the multi-attempt loop converges to passing tests within 3 attempts and verify intermediate failure-to-refinement transitions occurred.
